chapter_title: About the Virtue of Treating One's Slave kindly

hadith_entries.text:
742. Narrated by Abu-Zarr : 'I abused a man by calling
his mother bad names.' The Prophet said to me: "O Abu-
Zarr! Did you abuse him by calling his mother bad names?
You still have some characteristics of [the time of] ignorance
[i.e. pre-Islamic times]. Your slaves are your brothers and
Allah has put them under your command. So whoever has a
brother under his command should feed him from what he
eats and dress him from what he wears. Do not ask them
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
(slaves) to do things beyond their capacity (power) and if you
do so, then help them." (Bukhari)

743. Narrated Abu-Horaira : 'The Prophet said:
"When your servant brings your meal to you then if you do
not let him sit and share the meal with you, then he should at
least be given a mouthful or two of that meal as he has
prepared it.””(Bukhari)
